content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(3, 7, 2), (4, 9, 3), (5, 7, 6), (0, 8, 7), (4, 5, 9), (8, 9, 10), (7, 8, 14), (3, 8, 18), (5, 9, 19), (0, 4, 20), (2, 5, 24), (4, 6, 25), (7, 9, 28), (5, 8, 29)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 5 exactly at time 29.

First, we will scan each edge (u, v, t) and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node:

* Edge (3, 7, 2) has node 3 and time 2. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (4, 9, 3) has node 4 and time 3. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (5, 7, 6) has node 5 and time 6. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 8, 7) has node 0 and time 7. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (4, 5, 9) has node 4 and time 9. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (8, 9, 10) has node 8 and time 10. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (7, 8, 14) has node 7 and time 14. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (3, 8, 18) has node 3 and time 18. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (5, 9, 19) has node 5 and time 19. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (0, 4, 20) has node 0 and time 20. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (2, 5, 24) has node 2 and time 24. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (4, 6, 25) has node 4 and time 25. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (7, 9, 28) has node 7 and time 28. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (5, 8, 29) has node 5 and time 29. -> Add node 8.

Now we have the following nodes linked with node 5 at time 29:

* Node 7
* Node 9

The final answer is: [7, 9]
